Question ID: coding-S3-ahc024-topological-map
Title: S3. AHC024 / Topological Map

Problem:
Compress a colored city map onto a 50 x 50 grid while preserving all color-adjacency relations and connectivity constraints, and maximize empty space.

Grading target:
E + 1, where E is the number of empty cells.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Technical Specification: Topological Map Compression Optimization. 1. Problem Overview: The objective is to embed a colored topological map (represented as a graph or set of adjacency constraints) onto a discrete 2D grid of fixed dimensions, maximizing the number of unoccupied cells while strictly adhering to color-adjacency and connectivity rules. 2. Input Requirements: Map Topology: A set of colored regions/nodes V = {v1, v2, ..., vn}. Adjacency Constraints: A set of edges E_adj where (vi, vj) in E_adj implies that region vi and region vj must share at least one boundary (edge/side) in the grid. Connectivity Constraints: Each region vi must consist of a single, contiguous set of cells (4-connectivity or 8-connectivity, typically 4-connectivity in grid problems). Color Mapping: Each region vi is assigned a color Ci. 3. Output Requirements: Grid Configuration: A 50 x 50 matrix G, where G_r,c in {0, C1, C2, ..., Cn}. 0 represents an empty cell. Ci represents a cell belonging to region vi. 4. Constraints: Grid Boundary: 1 <= r <= 50, 1 <= c <= 50. Region Integrity: For every region vi, the set of cells Si = {(r, c) | G_r,c = Ci} must be non-empty and connected. Adjacency Preservation: If (vi, vj) in E_adj, there must exist at least one pair of cells (r1, c1) in Si and (r2, c2) in Sj such that they are adjacent in the grid. Exclusivity: A cell cannot belong to more than one region (Si intersection Sj = empty for i != j). 5. Objective Function: Maximize the number of empty cells E: Maximize Z = sum over r=1 to 50, sum over c=1 to 50 of [G_r,c == 0]. 6. Evaluation Metric: Score = E + 1. 7. Implementation Notes: This is a constrained layout optimization problem, similar to floorplanning or VLSI design. The solver must handle the trade-off between the compactness required to satisfy adjacencies and the expansion required to maximize empty space.
